title: silene borysthenica subsp. parviflora
intro: Silene borysthenica subsp. parviflora, also known as a type of carnation, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carnation family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help in wound healing and reducing pain. The plant has been found to have health benefits such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. Silene borysthenica subsp. parviflora is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
